careTips.watering: Water once a week. It is best to water when the top 2 inches of soil feel dry.
careTips.sunlight: Thrives in bright, indirect light. Direct sunlight can scorch the leaves.
careTips.fertilizing: Apply a liquid fertilizer once a month during spring and summer.

careTips.watering: Keep the topsoil lightly moist. Avoid letting water sit at the bottom of the pot.
careTips.sunlight: Prefers bright to medium indirect light. Growth slows down in low-light conditions.
careTips.fertilizing: Feed with a balanced indoor plant fertilizer every 6-8 weeks.

careTips.watering: Needs very little water. Water only when the soil has dried out completely.
careTips.sunlight: Can survive in low light, but thrives and grows faster in bright, indirect light.
careTips.fertilizing: Fertilizing twice a year (during spring and autumn) is sufficient.

careTips.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist. If leaves start drooping, it needs water.
careTips.sunlight: Needs bright, indirect light. Direct sun can cause flowers to wilt quickly.
careTips.fertilizing: Use a bloom-booster fertilizer once a month during the flowering season.

careTips.watering: Water deeply only when the soil is completely dry. Avoid getting water on the leaves.
careTips.sunlight: Requires at least 6 hours of bright sunlight or intense indirect light daily.
careTips.fertilizing: Use a specialized cactus or succulent fertilizer once a month during summer.
